concerns.text: FR-005 states 'VIF exceeding a specified threshold indicates moderate definitional dependency' and 'VIF exceeding a specified threshold indicates severe definitional dependency' - the same phrase 'a specified threshold' is used twice without differentiating moderate vs severe. Assumptions section specifies VIF > 5 for moderate and VIF > 10 for severe. FR-005 should be explicit about these threshold values for consistency.

FR-003 states validation pass/fail threshold is 'a high degree of match with reference values' while SC-012 explicitly quantifies this as '≥90% threshold'. The FR should be explicit about the 90% threshold to match SC-012 for cross-document consistency.

FR-010 states 'Partial results MUST be cached to disk after a specified number of consecutive failures' without specifying the number. SC-005 specifies 'after 3 consecutive failures'. FR-010 should explicitly state '3 consecutive failures' for consistency with SC-005.
